title: Golden Triangle Classic — Delhi, Agra & Jaipur
notes: India's most popular tourist circuit. Book hotels and Taj Mahal tickets well in advance during peak season (Nov-Feb). The Golden Triangle covers ~750 km total.

days.title: Arrival in Delhi — Mughal Grandeur
days.description: Arrive in New Delhi and explore iconic Mughal and colonial landmarks.

monuments.name: India Gate
monuments.notes: Great for morning photos; lawn area perfect for a stroll.

monuments.name: Humayun's Tomb
monuments.notes: Precursor to the Taj Mahal — a must-see for Mughal architecture.

monuments.name: Qutub Minar
monuments.notes: World's tallest brick minaret. Visit Iron Pillar inside the complex.

monuments.name: Iron Pillar
monuments.notes: Ancient iron pillar inside Qutub complex — remarkable 1600-year-old metallurgy.

transport.mode: Cab/Auto
transport.notes: Book a full-day cab (~₹1200-1500). Metro available between major spots.

tips: Carry water and sunscreen.
Taj Mahal is closed on Fridays.
Book tickets in advance on ASI website.

days.title: Delhi Deep Dive — Ancient Gates & Sacred Spaces
days.description: Explore more of Delhi's layered history from Mughal gateways to the serene Lotus Temple.

monuments.name: Ajmeri Gate
monuments.notes: Historic Mughal-era gate in Old Delhi.

monuments.name: Jamali Kamali Tomb
monuments.notes: Hidden gem in Mehrauli — peaceful and photogenic.

monuments.name: Lotus Temple
monuments.notes: No photography inside. Serene Bahá'í temple open to all faiths.

transport.mode: Metro + Auto
transport.notes: Yellow Line metro convenient for Old Delhi. Take auto for Mehrauli.

tips: Old Delhi is best explored in the morning before crowds build.
Dress modestly at Lotus Temple.

days.title: Drive to Agra — The Iconic Taj Mahal
days.description: Travel to Agra and witness the world's greatest monument to love.

monuments.name: Taj Mahal
monuments.notes: Arrive at sunrise — golden light on white marble is breathtaking. Pre-book tickets.

monuments.name: Fatehpur Sikri
monuments.notes: Akbar's abandoned red sandstone city — hauntingly beautiful.

monuments.name: Buland Darwaza
monuments.notes: The grand Victory Gate of Fatehpur Sikri — 54 metres tall.

transport.mode: Hired car Delhi-Agra (~3.5 hrs via Yamuna Expressway)
transport.notes: Diesel vehicles not allowed near Taj Mahal. Use official e-rickshaws.

tips: Taj Mahal is closed on Fridays.
Carry only small bags (no food, tripods) inside Taj complex.
Book e-rickshaw from parking to Taj gate.

days.title: Agra to Jaipur — Pink City Arrival
days.description: Morning at Fatehpur Sikri Fort then drive to Jaipur — the Pink City of Rajasthan.

monuments.name: Fatehpur Sikri Fort
monuments.notes: Walk the royal complex before heat sets in.

monuments.name: Hawa Mahal
monuments.notes: The Palace of Winds — best viewed from the street cafe opposite.

monuments.name: Albert Hall Museum
monuments.notes: Stunning collection of Rajasthani art, textiles, and manuscripts.

transport.mode: Hired car Agra-Jaipur (~4.5 hrs via NH21)
transport.notes: Rajasthan State Highway — comfortable and scenic.

tips: Hawa Mahal exterior is the photo spot — interior is small.
Book Chokhi Dhani in advance for a folk dinner experience.

days.title: Jaipur — Rajput Splendour & Departure
days.description: Explore the magnificent royal forts and palaces of Jaipur before returning to Delhi.

monuments.name: Isarlat Sargasooli
monuments.notes: Seven-storey tower offering panoramic views of Jaipur's old city.

transport.mode: Hired car Jaipur-Delhi (~5 hrs via NH48)
transport.notes: Delhi-Jaipur expressway — India's first 6-lane highway. Smooth drive.

tips: Start early to beat traffic.
Shop for blue pottery and block-print fabrics in Jaipur markets.
Delhi airport is 45-60 mins from city.

title: Sacred Buddhist Circuit — Bodh Gaya, Nalanda & Rajgir
notes: This circuit follows in the footsteps of Gautama Buddha and Emperor Ashoka. Best paired with a Varanasi extension. Gaya has the nearest airport (GAY).

days.title: Bodh Gaya — Where Buddha Attained Enlightenment
days.description: Arrive at the holiest Buddhist site in the world and experience deep spiritual peace.

monuments.name: Mahabodhi Temple
monuments.notes: UNESCO World Heritage site. Attend the morning prayer at 5:30 AM — a deeply moving experience.

monuments.name: Bodhi Tree
monuments.notes: Sit under the sacred fig tree and meditate where the Buddha found enlightenment.

monuments.name: Bhudha Temple
monuments.notes: Explore the surrounding monastery complex with temples from 12+ countries.

monuments.name: Vishnupad Temple
monuments.notes: 15 km away in Gaya — sacred Hindu temple with Vishnu's footprint.

transport.mode: Auto-rickshaw within Bodh Gaya; hired jeep to Gaya (30 min)

tips: Dress modestly — shoulders and knees covered.
Photography restrictions around the Bodhi Tree. Respect meditating pilgrims.
Best visited Oct-Mar; huge crowds on Buddha Purnima.

days.title: Nalanda & Rajgir — Ancient Learning & Peace
days.description: Visit the world's first university and the sacred hills of Rajgir.

monuments.name: Nalanda
monuments.notes: UNESCO Heritage site — walk through the ruins of the world's greatest ancient university.

monuments.name: Vishwa Santi Stupa
monuments.notes: Take the ropeway up Ratnagiri Hill for panoramic views. 4 golden Buddha statues on the stupa.

transport.mode: Hired cab Bodh Gaya to Rajgir (~2 hrs, 80 km); auto within Rajgir

tips: Combine Nalanda and Rajgir in one day — they're 12 km apart.
Ropeway to Vishwa Santi Stupa: ₹50 return. Avoid Sundays (crowded).
Hot springs at Rajgir are worth a dip.

days.title: Pawapuri & Vaishali — Jain & Buddhist Heritage
days.description: Visit the sacred Jain shrine of Jal Mandir and the ancient Buddhist city of Vaishali.

monuments.name: Jal Mandir
monuments.notes: Serene white temple on a lotus lake — Lord Mahavira's nirvana site.

monuments.name: Ashoka Pillar
monuments.notes: Vaishali — one of India's finest surviving Ashoka pillars near the ancient stupa.

transport.mode: Hired cab — Rajgir → Pawapuri (35 km) → Vaishali (80 km) → Patna (55 km)

tips: Pawapuri lake is stunning in winter with blooming lotus.
Vaishali is the birthplace of Lord Mahavira and also where Buddha gave his last sermon.
Combine both sites efficiently as they lie on Rajgir-Patna route.

days.title: Sasaram — The Afghan Tomb & Departure
days.description: Visit the magnificent mausoleum of Sher Shah Suri before departing.

monuments.name: Tomb of Sher Shah Sori
monuments.notes: One of India's finest tombs — rises 46m from an artificial lake. Often called India's forgotten masterpiece.

transport.mode: Hired cab Patna-Sasaram (~3.5 hrs, 160 km); onward to Varanasi (~2 hrs)

tips: Sasaram is en route between Patna and Varanasi — ideal stop.
The tomb is accessible via a causeway over the lake.
Combine with Varanasi for a 5-day Bihar-UP circuit.

title: Rajasthan Royal Odyssey — Forts, Palaces & Desert
notes: Rajasthan is India's most popular tourist state. This route covers its most dramatic heritage — from the tragedy of Chittorgarh to the romance of Udaipur. Hire a private car for comfort.

days.title: Jaipur — The Pink City
days.description: Explore Jaipur's iconic Pink City architecture, markets, and landmarks.

monuments.name: Hawa Mahal
monuments.notes: Best photographed from Windview Café across the street. Go early for best shots.

monuments.name: Albert Hall Museum
monuments.notes: Stunning Indo-Saracenic building housing Rajasthani art and archaeology.

monuments.name: Isarlat Sargasooli
monuments.notes: Climb 7 floors for the best aerial view of the walled pink city.

transport.mode: Auto/cab; hire a cycle-rickshaw in the walled city

tips: Start with markets in the morning (Johari Bazaar for gems, Bapu for crafts).
Jaipur Literature Festival is held in Jan/Feb — a cultural highlight.

days.title: Jaipur Day 2 — Amber Fort & Surroundings
days.description: Explore Amber Fort complex and more of the Jaipur region's heritage.

monuments.name: Isarlat Sargasooli
monuments.notes: Sunrise view of the walled city from the top.

transport.mode: Cab to Amber Fort (~11 km from city)

tips: Amber Fort elephant rides have been restricted — opt for jeep. Book online.
Light and Sound show at Amber Fort at 7 PM — spectacular.
Jaigarh Fort above Amber Fort is worth a separate visit.

days.title: Drive to Chittorgarh — Epic Rajput Citadel
days.description: Drive to the ancient capital of Mewar — home to India's greatest fort.

monuments.name: Chittorgarh Fort
monuments.notes: Start at Vijay Stambha (Tower of Victory) and work down to Padmini Palace. Spread over 700 acres.

monuments.name: Chittorgarh
monuments.notes: The fort complex itself has 65 historic structures — take a guide.

monuments.name: Padmini Lake Palace
monuments.notes: Romantic lake palace — stand at the famous mirror where Alauddin Khilji supposedly glimpsed Queen Padmini.

transport.mode: Hired car Jaipur-Chittorgarh (~4 hrs, 310 km via NH48 + NH27)

tips: Chittorgarh Fort is massive — hire a guide or e-vehicle for the 5 km internal circuit.
The light and sound show at Vijay Stambha is excellent.
Mewar Festival (March) is a brilliant time to visit.

days.title: Chittorgarh to Udaipur — City of Lakes
days.description: Drive to Udaipur — the most romantic city in Rajasthan.

monuments.name: City Palace
monuments.notes: The largest palace complex in Rajasthan — stunning lakeside architecture with panoramic lake views.

transport.mode: Hired car Chittorgarh-Udaipur (~2 hrs, 120 km)

tips: City Palace Museum ticket is separate from the hotel section.
Book a sunset boat ride on Lake Pichola.
Udaipur has outstanding heritage hotels including Taj Lake Palace (on an island).

days.title: Udaipur Day 2 — Royal Palaces & Lakes
days.description: Full day exploring Udaipur's magnificent lakes and palaces.

monuments.name: City Palace
monuments.notes: Second visit to complete the museum and reach the rooftop terraces.

monuments.name: Padmini Lake Palace
monuments.notes: Morning visit to Chittorgarh area before returning.

meals.dinner: Upre by 1559 AD (rooftop Lake Pichola view)

transport.mode: Auto/cycle-rickshaw in Udaipur city

tips: Sunset from Sajjangarh (Monsoon Palace) is outstanding.
Take a boat to Jagmandir Island — a floating palace.
Book hotels near the old city for the best experience.

days.title: Leisure & Culture — Crafts & Folk Arts
days.description: Explore local crafts, miniature painting workshops and folk culture.

transport.mode: Walking in old city

tips: Visit Shilpgram Crafts Village on weekends for live demonstrations.
Attend a Dharohar Folk Dance Show at Bagore Ki Haveli (7:30 PM daily).
Shop for miniature paintings, silver jewellery, and Bandhani fabric.

days.title: Departure from Udaipur
days.description: Morning at leisure, then transfer to Udaipur airport.

transport.mode: Pre-booked cab to airport (15 km)

tips: Udaipur Airport (UDR) has good connectivity to Delhi and Mumbai.
Book airport transfer in advance.
Carry Rajasthani souvenirs — mini palaces, blue pottery, lac bangles.

title: Maharashtra Heritage Trail — Caves, Forts & Coastal Gems
notes: Maharashtra's UNESCO trifecta — Ajanta, Ellora, and CST — make this one of India's richest heritage circuits. Avoid monsoon for cave visits (landslide risk).

days.title: Pune — Mahatma Gandhi's Prison & Colonial Legacy
days.description: Explore Pune's historic sites connected to India's freedom struggle.

monuments.name: Aga Khan Palace
monuments.notes: Gandhi's memorial prison. Humble yet deeply moving. Kasturba Gandhi is buried in the garden.

transport.mode: Auto/cab in Pune city

tips: Pune has a vibrant café culture — explore Camp area in the evening.
Osho International Meditation Resort is nearby for those interested.
Peshwa-era wadas and Aga Khan Palace make for a full day.

days.title: Mumbai — India's Maximum City
days.description: Explore Bollywood's home, the Gateway of India, and colonial-era grandeur.

monuments.name: Gateway of India
monuments.notes: Iconic basalt arch facing the Arabian Sea. Take a ferry to Elephanta Caves from here.

monuments.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus
monuments.notes: UNESCO-listed Victorian Gothic railway station — one of the finest in the world. Still operational.

monuments.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Statue
monuments.notes: Proposed monument to the Maratha king — currently one of the world's largest statues under development.

transport.mode: Train Pune-Mumbai (~3.5 hrs Deccan Express); local train/cab in Mumbai

tips: Gateway of India is free but crowded — go morning or evening.
Marine Drive (Queen's Necklace) is a 15-min walk from CST.
Book Elephanta Caves ferry from Gateway Pier.

days.title: Fly/Drive to Aurangabad — Gateway to UNESCO Caves
days.description: Travel to Aurangabad and explore the extraordinary Ellora Caves.

monuments.name: Ellora Caves
monuments.notes: 32 rock-cut cave temples. Kailasa Temple (Cave 16) is the world's largest rock-cut structure. Spectacular.

transport.mode: Flight Mumbai-Aurangabad (~45 min) or overnight bus (~8 hrs)

tips: Ellora is closed on Tuesdays.
Kailasa Temple alone needs 2 hours — it's mind-blowing.
Aurangabad has excellent cave hotels and MTDC resort nearby.

days.title: Ajanta Caves — Ancient Buddhist Masterpieces
days.description: Drive to Ajanta and marvel at 2,000-year-old Buddhist cave paintings.

monuments.name: Ajanta Caves
monuments.notes: UNESCO Heritage caves. Paintings in Cave 1, 2, 16, 17 are finest. Bring a torch.

transport.mode: Hired cab Aurangabad to Ajanta (102 km, ~2.5 hrs each way)

tips: Ajanta is closed on Mondays.
Special torches available at ticket counter to illuminate cave paintings.
Start early — last entry is at 4:30 PM.
Combine Ajanta + Ellora on separate days — both need serious time.

days.title: Departure from Aurangabad
days.description: Morning optional visit before departure.

transport.mode: Cab to Aurangabad Airport or railway station

tips: Aurangabad airport (IXU) connects to Mumbai and Delhi.
Buy Paithani silk sarees and Himroo shawls — Aurangabad specialties.
Bibi Ka Maqbara (Mini Taj) worth a morning stop before departure.

title: Goa — Portuguese Legacy, Forts & Sun-Kissed Beaches
notes: Goa is India's most beloved beach destination. December-January is peak season — book hotels 2-3 months ahead. Monsoon (Jun-Sep) closes some beach clubs but is lush and green.

days.title: Old Goa — UNESCO Churches & Colonial Heritage
days.description: Explore the magnificent churches and cathedrals of Old Goa — the Rome of the East.

monuments.name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
monuments.notes: Houses the preserved body of St. Francis Xavier. UNESCO listed. Baroque masterpiece.

transport.mode: Scooter rental (₹300-400/day) or cab

tips: Old Goa churches are all within walking distance of each other.
Dress modestly for churches — no shorts or sleeveless.
Se Cathedral nearby is the largest church in Asia.

days.title: North Goa — Forts, Beaches & Bohemian Vibes
days.description: Explore North Goa's iconic Portuguese forts and vibrant beach culture.

monuments.name: Chapora Fort
monuments.notes: Famous Dil Chahta Hai fort — offers stunning panoramic views of Vagator Beach.

monuments.name: Fort Aguada
monuments.notes: Best-preserved Portuguese fort in Goa with a 4-storey lighthouse. Overlooking Sinquerim Beach.

transport.mode: Scooter or hired cab — North Goa sights are 10-20 km apart

tips: Chapora Fort is best at sunrise/sunset — the view over Vagator is stunning.
Fort Aguada has both the fort on top and a beach section below.
Anjuna Flea Market on Wednesdays is a classic Goa experience.

days.title: South Goa — Daman Extension
days.description: Drive south to Daman for Portuguese colonial forts and quiet beaches.

monuments.name: Daman
monuments.notes: Explore Moti Daman Fort, the Lighthouse, and Jampore Beach in this quiet Portuguese enclave.

meals.dinner: Back in Goa — La Plage, Aswem

transport.mode: Hired cab Goa to Daman (~3 hrs, 170 km); return same day

tips: Daman is separate from Goa but shares Portuguese colonial heritage.
Diu is 3 hrs from Daman — consider extending for the Diu Museum.
Daman has quiet, clean beaches — far less crowded than Goa.

days.title: Leisure & Departure
days.description: Morning leisure, last beach visit, departure.

transport.mode: Cab to Goa Airport (Dabolim or Mopa)

tips: Goa has two airports — confirm which one your flight uses.
Buy cashews, Goan spices, and feni (local spirit) as souvenirs.
Book airport transfer in advance especially during peak season (Dec-Jan).

title: South India Temple Grandeur — Karnataka, Tamil Nadu & Kerala
notes: South India's temple architecture is unlike anything in the world. This circuit blends Dravidian temples, Vijayanagara ruins, and Mughal-era coastal forts across 3 states.

days.title: Hampi — Lost City of the Vijayanagara Empire
days.description: Arrive at Hampi — one of the most extraordinary archaeological sites in the world.

monuments.name: Hampi
monuments.notes: UNESCO Heritage site. Take a guide — 50+ monuments scattered over 25 sq km. Virupaksha Temple, Vittala Temple, and Elephant Stables are highlights.

transport.mode: Bicycle rental (₹100/day) or mopeds — best way to explore Hampi

tips: Hampi is divided by the Tungabhadra River — Coracle boats cross between sides.
Vittala Temple's stone chariot and musical pillars are unmissable.
Hampi Utsav festival in November is a cultural highlight.

days.title: Hampi Day 2 & Drive to Mysore
days.description: Complete Hampi exploration and drive to the royal city of Mysore.

monuments.name: Mysore Palace
monuments.notes: The grandest palace in Karnataka — especially stunning during Dasara (Oct) when 100,000 lights illuminate it.

transport.mode: Hired cab Hampi-Mysore (~5 hrs, 250 km)

tips: Mysore Palace: Sunday and national holidays have illumination from 7-7:45 PM.
The Dasara festival (Oct) is the best time to visit Mysore.
Book heritage stay in Mysore for a regal experience.

days.title: Mysore — Royal Heritage & Wildlife
days.description: Full day exploring Mysore's royal and cultural heritage.

monuments.name: Mysore Palace
monuments.notes: Explore the interior with Durbar Hall, golden throne, and royal art.

transport.mode: Auto/cycle-rickshaw in city

tips: Brindavan Gardens illumination show at 7:30 PM is magical.
Mysore is famous for silk sarees, sandalwood products, and agarbatti.
Namdroling Monastery (Golden Temple) 70 km away — Buddha temple with stunning Tibetan art.

days.title: Fly to Chennai, Drive to Thanjavur — Temple Town
days.description: Fly to Chennai and drive to Thanjavur — home of the magnificent Brihadeshwara Temple.

monuments.name: Brihadeshwara Temple
monuments.notes: UNESCO Heritage temple. The 216 ft vimana (tower) casts no shadow at noon. Built 1000 years ago — engineering marvel.

monuments.name: Tanjavur Temple
monuments.notes: Explore the surrounding temple complex and Saraswati Mahal Library.

transport.mode: Flight Mysore/Bangalore-Chennai (~1 hr); cab Chennai-Thanjavur (~4 hrs, 330 km)

tips: Thanjavur Brihadeshwara Temple is best in morning light — golden stone glows brilliantly.
The temple is a working Shaivite shrine — follow dress code.
Thanjavur is famous for Tanjore painting, bronze idols, and veena.

days.title: Thanjavur to Kasaragod — Bekal Fort
days.description: Drive to Kerala's northernmost district to visit the magnificent seaside Bekal Fort.

monuments.name: Bekal Fort
monuments.notes: India's largest fort in Kerala — spectacular views of the Arabian Sea. Keerthi Chakra shape when viewed from above.

meals.dinner: Bekal Boat Stay or Vivanta Bekal (resort)

transport.mode: Hired cab or bus Thanjavur-Kasaragod (~8 hrs, 450 km via Calicut)

tips: Bekal Fort at sunset is magical — sea surrounds three sides.
Bekal Beach next to the fort is clean and uncrowded.
Bekal is best base for exploring North Kerala's heritage.

days.title: Departure from Mangalore / Kozhikode
days.description: Morning at leisure, then depart from nearest airport.

transport.mode: Cab to Mangalore (IXE) or Kozhikode (CCJ) airport

tips: Kozhikode is famous for Malabar biryani — don't miss it.
Buy Kashmiri handicrafts, sandalwood products, and Kerala spices.
Bekal-Kozhikode drive along NH66 coastal highway is scenic.

title: Wonders of the World — Grand International Tour
notes: This premium itinerary covers 5 of the 7 New Wonders of the World plus UNESCO iconic landmarks. Book flights, Taj Mahal, Eiffel Tower, Colosseum, and Burj Khalifa tickets months in advance.

days.title: Paris — The Iron Lady
days.description: Arrive in Paris and visit the world's most visited monument.

monuments.name: Eiffel Tower
monuments.notes: Book tickets online 2-3 months in advance. Top floor offers 360° view of Paris. Magical at night.

meals.dinner: Jules Verne (inside Eiffel Tower — book 3 months ahead)

transport.mode: Metro Line 6 to Trocadéro; RER C to Champ de Mars

tips: Book Eiffel Tower tickets online at least 6-8 weeks in advance.
Best photos from Trocadéro Gardens (across the river).
Night illumination runs every hour after sunset — spectacular.

days.title: Paris Day 2 — Louvre & Montmartre
days.description: Explore world's greatest art museum and the bohemian hilltop of Montmartre.

transport.mode: Metro

tips: Louvre has 35,000 works — pre-plan which wings to visit.
Mona Lisa is smaller than expected — arrive early to avoid crowds.
Montmartre's Sacré-Cœur offers another stunning Paris panorama.

days.title: Rome — The Eternal City
days.description: Fly to Rome and visit the greatest amphitheatre ever built.

monuments.name: Roman Colosseum
monuments.notes: UNESCO Heritage site. Book skip-the-line tickets online. Gladiator-era engineering marvel.

meals.lunch: At Rome airport or café near Colosseum

transport.mode: Flight Paris-Rome (~2 hrs); Metro B to Colosseo

tips: Book Colosseum + Roman Forum + Palatine Hill combo ticket.
The underground (hypogeum) requires a separate premium ticket.
Trevi Fountain and Spanish Steps are walking distance from city centre.

days.title: Rome Day 2 — Vatican & Ancient Ruins
days.description: Explore Vatican City and more of Rome's ancient splendour.

transport.mode: Walking + Metro

tips: Vatican Museums require advance booking — Sistine Chapel is inside.
Pantheon is free — arrive early.
Roman Forum connects to Palatine Hill — allow 3-4 hours.

days.title: Cairo — The Last Wonder of the Ancient World
days.description: Fly to Cairo and witness the only surviving ancient wonder of the world.

monuments.name: Pyramids of Giza
monuments.notes: The Great Pyramid, Pyramid of Khafre, Pyramid of Menkaure, and the Great Sphinx. Hire a licensed guide.

meals.lunch: Koshary Abou Tarek (famous Egyptian street food)

transport.mode: Flight Rome-Cairo (~3 hrs); taxi to Giza (~1 hr from downtown Cairo)

tips: Camel rides near Pyramids — negotiate price firmly before mounting.
Sound and Light Show at Pyramids nightly — spectacular.
Avoid unofficial guides at the gate — only use licensed ones.

days.title: Cairo Day 2 — Egyptian Museum & Old Cairo
days.description: Explore the world's finest collection of ancient Egyptian artefacts.

meals.dinner: Naguib Mahfouz Café, Khan el-Khalili (historic bazaar)

transport.mode: Taxi/Uber in Cairo

tips: Egyptian Museum has Tutankhamun's gold mask — unmissable.
Khan el-Khalili bazaar is a sensory overload — bargain hard.
Book the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM) if open — largest in the world.

days.title: Dubai — Touching the Clouds
days.description: Fly to Dubai and ascend the world's tallest building.

monuments.name: Burj Khalifa
monuments.notes: Book the 124th or 148th floor 'At the Top' tickets online. Sunset visit gives day + night views.

meals.lunch: At Dubai Mall (world's largest mall, connected to Burj Khalifa)

transport.mode: Flight Cairo-Dubai (~3 hrs); Metro Red Line to Burj Khalifa/Dubai Mall

tips: Book Burj Khalifa tickets well in advance — sunset slots sell out fast.
Dubai Fountain show at Dubai Mall every 30 min from 6 PM — free and spectacular.
Dubai Frame and Museum of the Future are alternative modern icons.

days.title: Dubai Day 2 — Gold Souk & Desert Safari
days.description: Explore traditional Dubai before a classic desert safari experience.

transport.mode: Taxi/Metro

tips: Gold Souk: buy gold by weight — it's among cheapest in world.
Desert safari: 4WD dune bashing, camel ride, falconry, and BBQ dinner.
Old Dubai (Deira, Bur Dubai) and the Abra (water taxi) are authentic old-city experiences.

days.title: Fly to Agra — The Taj Mahal
days.description: Fly to India and witness the greatest monument to love — the Taj Mahal.

monuments.name: Taj Mahal
monuments.notes: The world's most beautiful building. Sunrise visit is the most iconic — white marble glows in pink-gold light.

transport.mode: Flight Dubai-Delhi (~3 hrs); cab Delhi-Agra (~3.5 hrs via Yamuna Expressway)

tips: Book Taj Mahal tickets at asiagraticketing.nic.in — no same-day cash tickets now.
The Taj is most beautiful at sunrise and in full moon nights (special tickets).
Stay at Oberoi Amarvilas for a Taj-view room — a bucket list experience.

days.title: Agra to Delhi — Departure
days.description: Morning at Fatehpur Sikri, then fly home from Delhi.

monuments.name: Fatehpur Sikri
monuments.notes: Akbar's abandoned red sandstone ghost city — on the way from Agra to Delhi.

monuments.name: Buland Darwaza
monuments.notes: The magnificent Victory Gate of Fatehpur Sikri.

meals.dinner: At Delhi airport or home city

transport.mode: Cab Agra-Fatehpur Sikri (~40 min) then Sikri-Delhi (~3.5 hrs)

tips: Fatehpur Sikri is 40 km from Agra — perfect en-route stop.
Check in 3 hours before international departure from Delhi T3.
This 10-day itinerary covers 5 countries and 4 continents of heritage.

title: Nawabi Grandeur — Hyderabad & Lucknow
notes: Hyderabad and Lucknow are India's two great Nawabi cities — where Mughal refinement met regional culture to create unique food, language, and architecture. Biryani in both cities is a pilgrimage.

days.title: Hyderabad — The City of Pearls
days.description: Explore the magnificent Nizam-era monuments of Hyderabad.

monuments.name: Charminar
monuments.notes: The iconic four-minaret monument in the heart of the old city — surrounded by bustling bazaars.

monuments.name: Falaknuma Palace
monuments.notes: Reserve in advance for heritage lunch/dinner/tour — one of India's most opulent palaces. Jacquar III furniture, Crystal dining table, and Paigah tombs.

transport.mode: Auto/cab; Charminar is best explored on foot

tips: Charminar area: Laad Bazaar for bangles, Pathar Gatti for pearls.
Book Falaknuma Palace heritage dinner well in advance (₹5,000+ per person).
Chowmahalla Palace nearby is another Nizam-era gem.

days.title: Hyderabad Day 2 — Golconda Fort & Ramoji
days.description: Explore Golconda Fort and more of Hyderabad's layered heritage.

transport.mode: Cab within Hyderabad

tips: Golconda Fort: Sound & Light show at 7 PM is excellent.
Ramoji Film City is a full-day theme park for Bollywood fans.
Salar Jung Museum has one of the world's largest one-man art collections.

days.title: Fly to Lucknow — The City of Nawabs
days.description: Fly to Lucknow and explore its magnificent Nawabi imambara complexes.

monuments.name: Bara Imambara
monuments.notes: Largest hall without supporting beams — architectural wonder. The Bhulbhulaiya (maze) on the roof is a highlight.

monuments.name: Chhota Imambara
monuments.notes: Palace of Lights — stunning Zardozi chandeliers and European-style architecture. More ornate than Bara Imambara.

transport.mode: Flight Hyderabad-Lucknow (~1.5 hrs); cab from airport

tips: Both imambaras are best explored with an official guide.
The Bhulbhulaiya (maze) at Bara Imambara: stay with the group to avoid getting lost.
Lucknow has the most refined form of Hindustani music, dance, and cuisine.

days.title: Lucknow Culture & Departure
days.description: Explore Lucknow's famous food culture and heritage before departure.

transport.mode: Auto/cab; walking in Hazratganj

tips: Tunday Kababi's galauti kebab is among India's finest foods.
Chikan embroidery work is Lucknow's most famous craft — buy it in Hazratganj.
Ambedkar Park and Residency are other heritage spots.
Lucknow Airport (LKO) connects to major Indian cities.
